concept: Reptile / Scaled Cold-Blooded Creature / Ancient Lineage Being
description: A cold-blooded, scaly animal that typically lays eggs on land. 'Keshoa' names the ancient lineage of snakes, lizards, turtles, and crocodilians—beings whose ancestors walked the earth long before mammals, carrying deep time in their patient presence.
sound_symbolism: The word moves with reptilian patience. 'Ke' begins with velar contact, scales touching earth. Then 'sho' /ʃo/ slides with hushing stealth, the reptile's quiet movement. Finally 'a' opens to stillness, the patient wait.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo keshoa' (the reptile). Takes the animate classifier 'himo' when counted. Includes snakes, lizards, turtles, crocodiles. Often associated with patience, ancient wisdom, and earth connection.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Reptiles represent ancient lineages and earth connection—beings that bask in sun and move with patient deliberation.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The reptile's stillness and patience offer a teaching—not all action requires speed, not all presence requires motion.
